text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 21 September 2025

 Dr Aisha Karim 
 City Student Health Centre 

 45 Scholar Lane, Central Campus, London WC1N 3AB 
Dear  Dr Aisha Karim 
 Daniel Morgan , DOB:  14-02-2004 , Hospital No:  H739205  NHS No.  9035176241 
 Flat 12, Archer House, 3 Pavilion Road, London SW1X 0BA  

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent unprovoked seizures under ongoing assessment; prior routine EEG normal; MRI pending

Background: University esports club lead with prolonged screen time and irregular sleep patterns. No known family history of seizures. No significant head injury. Does not drive. Caffeine use variable around competitive events. Reports stress around tournaments and coursework peaks.

Previous investigations: Routine EEG 2023 reported normal. No prior prolonged monitoring. Basic bloods earlier this year reportedly normal (patient-supplied). MRI has not yet been completed; re-requested today.

Current medication: None for seizures. Uses occasional ibuprofen for tension-type headaches. No recreational drugs; occasional energy drinks around late-night matches.

Allergies: NKDA.

Clinical review: I saw  Daniel Morgan  in clinic today, unaccompanied. He reports recurrent events over the past 12 months characterised by brief unilateral limb jerks without loss of awareness, sometimes preceded by fatigue after late-night practice sessions. He has kept a simple diary over the last eight weeks documenting one or two focal clonic per week. Events tend to cluster after nights with <5 hours of sleep and high screen exposure. No tongue bite, no incontinence, and recovery is rapid though he feels mentally foggy for 10–15 minutes afterwards. No clear morning predominance. No known triggers beyond sleep deprivation and sustained visual strain. No prior antiseizure treatment trial.

Examination: Neurological examination today was normal. Cardiovascular and general exam unremarkable. BMI within normal range.

Risk discussion: We discussed lifestyle contributors including irregular sleep, prolonged continuous screen time, and stimulant beverages around esports competitions. He understands SUDEP and general safety guidance in broad terms; we agreed to revisit risk once further investigations are available. He does not currently drive and is aware to inform DVLA should the diagnosis be confirmed and per current guidance.

Plan:
- Investigations: MRI brain with epilepsy protocol requested; sleep-deprived EEG arranged; consider ambulatory EEG if initial studies non-diagnostic.
- Self-management: Maintain a detailed seizure diary, including sleep hours, screen-time blocks, caffeine/energy drink intake, and event features and duration.
- Lifestyle: Aim for consistent sleep schedule, regular breaks from screens during practice, hydration, and reduction of high-caffeine energy drinks, particularly overnight sessions.
- Safety: Briefly reviewed general safety around water, heights, and operating machinery. Provided written information.
- Follow-up: Nurse-led telephone review in 6 weeks to evaluate frequency pattern and diary quality; clinic review in 3 months or sooner if events escalate or change character.

I will write to you with results and any change in management once investigations are complete. Please let me know if there are concerns in the interim.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: he has kept a simple diary over the last eight weeks documenting one or two focal clonic per week.
one or two focal clonic per week

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter describes recurrent focal clonic events. It states: "he has kept a simple diary over the last eight weeks documenting one or two focal clonic per week." This provides an explicit numeric range of weekly frequency. No other higher frequency is given. Therefore, the highest/current frequency is 1 to 2 per week.
seizure_frequency_number: he has kept a simple diary over the last eight weeks documenting one or two focal clonic per week.
reference: one or two focal clonic per week
